title: Crunchy Pepperly Croatian Snapper Stir-fry
description: A romantic croatian dish featuring snapper fish with a spicy and crunchy twist, perfect for an evening meal.

ingredients.name: Red Peppers
ingredients.notes: Peeled and deseeded for a spicy kick

ingredients.name: Salt and Pepper
ingredients.notes: Seasoning for the fish and vegetables

ingredients.name: Spices
ingredients.amount: 1 tsp, ground red pepper flakes (or to taste)

instructions: Season the snapper fish fillets with salt and pepper on both sides.
In a large skillet or wok, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add chopped onions and garlic; sauté for about 3 minutes until translucent.
Add diced red peppers and green peppers to the skillet. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.

Gently lay the snapper fish fillets in the skillet, skin side down. Pour in enough water to cover half of the fish fillets. Place the skillet into a preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes or until cooked through.
While the fish cooks, chop fresh basil leaves and set aside.
When done cooking, remove the snapper fish from the skillet onto a plate. Let it rest while you prepare the pepper mixture.
Pour off any excess water from the skillet and add ground red pepper flakes to the remaining oil in the skillet. Stir well until fragrant.
Add chopped basil leaves into the skillet with the peppers, mixing gently for about 1-2 minutes. The heat should be low at this stage to avoid burning.
Return the cooked snapper fish fillets back into their pan and stir it through the mixed vegetables and peppers.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course, garnished with extra basil leaves if desired.
